Feature: Learning Collision Detection and Resolution
  As a workspace owner creating or approving learnings,
  I want the system to detect conflicts with existing learnings, policies, and decisions,
  so I do not accidentally introduce contradictory behavioral rules.

  Background:
    Given Tomas Eriksson owns workspace "Brain Development"
    And the embedding service is available

  # --- No Collision (Clean Path) ---

  Scenario: Learning created with no collisions detected
    Given no active learnings exist for the Architect agent
    And no policies reference caching technology choices
    When Tomas creates a learning "Always recommend Valkey for caching"
    And the collision check runs against learnings, policies, and decisions
    And no results exceed the similarity threshold
    Then the learning is activated immediately with status "active"
    And no collision warning is shown

  # --- Learning-to-Learning Collision ---

  Scenario: New learning conflicts with existing active learning
    Given an active learning exists for the coding agent:
      | text                                              | type        |
      | Use raw SQL queries for performance-critical paths | instruction |
    When Tomas creates a learning "Always use Prisma ORM for database access"
    And the collision check finds similarity 0.83 with the existing learning
    Then a collision warning is displayed with:
      | field            | value                                            |
      | new_learning     | Always use Prisma ORM for database access         |
      | existing_learning| Use raw SQL queries for performance-critical paths |
      | similarity       | 0.83                                             |
      | collision_type   | contradicts                                       |
    And the warning shows resolution options: Create Anyway, Edit New, Dismiss Old, Cancel

  Scenario: Collision resolved by editing the new learning
    Given a collision warning is displayed between:
      | new      | Always use Prisma ORM for database access         |
      | existing | Use raw SQL queries for performance-critical paths |
    When Tomas selects "Edit New"
    And Tomas changes the text to "Use Prisma ORM for non-performance-critical database access"
    Then the collision check re-runs with the modified text
    And if no new collisions are found the learning is activated

  Scenario: Collision resolved by dismissing the old learning
    Given a collision warning is displayed between:
      | new      | Always use Prisma ORM for database access         |
      | existing | Use raw SQL queries for performance-critical paths |
    When Tomas selects "Dismiss Old"
    Then the existing learning status changes to "dismissed"
    And the existing learning has a dismissed_at timestamp
    And the new learning is activated with status "active"

  Scenario: Creating learning despite collision
    Given a collision warning is displayed
    When Tomas selects "Create Anyway"
    Then both learnings remain active
    And the agent receives both learnings in its system prompt
    And the system prompt notes the potential conflict

  # --- Duplicate Detection ---

  Scenario: Near-duplicate learning detected
    Given an active learning exists for the Architect agent:
      | text                                                     | type       |
      | Always recommend Valkey for caching, never Redis         | constraint |
    When Tomas creates a learning "Never suggest Redis for caching, always use Valkey"
    And the collision check finds similarity 0.94
    Then a duplicate warning is displayed
    And the warning says "This is very similar to an existing active learning"
    And the resolution options are: Merge, Keep Both, Cancel

  # --- Policy Collision (Hard Block) ---

  Scenario: Learning contradicts active policy
    Given an active policy exists in the workspace:
      | title                         | rule_effect | rule_condition                     |
      | All Code Changes Require Review | deny       | code_merge when review_count < 1   |
    When Tomas creates a learning "Skip code review for small changes under 10 lines"
    And the collision check detects semantic conflict with the policy
    Then a policy collision is displayed as a hard block
    And the warning explains the conflicting policy rule
    And the only options are: View Policy, Cancel
    And the learning is not activated

  Scenario: Policy collision message guides to resolution
    Given a learning was blocked due to policy collision
    When the policy collision warning is displayed
    Then the warning includes: "Modify or supersede the policy first, then create the learning."
    And a "View Policy" action navigates to the policy details

  # --- Decision Collision (Informational) ---

  Scenario: Learning reinforces confirmed decision
    Given a confirmed decision exists:
      | summary                        | status    |
      | Standardize on PostgreSQL      | confirmed |
    When Tomas creates a learning "Prefer PostgreSQL over MySQL for new services"
    And the collision check finds similarity 0.88 with the decision
    And the collision is classified as "reinforces"
    Then an informational note is displayed: "This learning reinforces decision: Standardize on PostgreSQL"
    And the learning is activated normally

  Scenario: Learning contradicts confirmed decision
    Given a confirmed decision exists:
      | summary                        | status    |
      | Standardize on PostgreSQL      | confirmed |
    When Tomas creates a learning "Use MySQL for all new services"
    And the collision check finds similarity 0.85 with contradicting polarity
    Then a decision collision warning is displayed
    And the warning says "This learning may conflict with confirmed decision: Standardize on PostgreSQL"
    And the options are: Create Anyway, Edit, Cancel

  # --- Priority Weighting in Collision Resolution ---

  Scenario: Human learning overrides agent-suggested learning of same type
    Given an active agent-suggested learning exists:
      | text                    | type        | suggested_by |
      | Always use semicolons   | instruction | observer     |
    When Tomas creates a learning "Do not enforce semicolons, rely on auto-formatting"
    And the collision check finds they contradict
    Then the collision warning notes "Your learning (human) will take precedence over the agent-suggested learning"
    And the warning suggests "Dismiss the agent-suggested learning?"

  Scenario: Constraint takes precedence over instruction
    Given an active instruction learning exists:
      | text                                    | type        |
      | Prefer tabs for indentation             | instruction |
    When Tomas creates a constraint "Never use tabs. All files must use 2-space indentation."
    And the collision check finds they contradict
    Then the collision warning notes "Constraint takes precedence over instruction"
    And the warning suggests "Dismiss the instruction?"

  # --- Error Handling ---

  Scenario: Embedding service unavailable during collision check
    Given the embedding service is unavailable
    When Tomas creates a learning "Always validate input parameters"
    Then the learning is created with status "active"
    And an observation is logged: "Learning created without collision check due to embedding service unavailability"
    And a deferred collision check is queued

  @property
  Scenario: Collision detection semantic accuracy
    Given the collision detection system is processing learnings
    Then learnings with semantic similarity above 0.75 are flagged for review
    And learnings with similarity above 0.90 are flagged as near-duplicates
    And learnings with opposite sentiment polarity are classified as "contradicts"
    And learnings with same sentiment polarity are classified as "overlaps" or "reinforces"

content.content:
1→# US-AL-004: Learning Governance Feed Cards
     2→
     3→## Problem
     4→Tomas Eriksson's workspace has accumulated 3 pending learning suggestions from the Observer and PM agents this week. He has no way to see them -- they exist in the database but do not surface in his governance feed. Without visibility, agent-suggested learnings pile up unreviewed, and the agents never benefit from the wisdom they detected.
     5→
     6→## Who
     7→- Workspace owner | Reviewing governance feed daily | Wants to efficiently approve, edit, or dismiss pending learnings
     8→
     9→## Job Story Trace
    10→- **Job 4**: Learning Governance and Curation
    11→- **When** agents have suggested behavioral learnings, **I want to** review them in my governance feed with evidence, **so I can** approve valuable rules and dismiss low-quality ones efficiently.
    12→
    13→## Solution
    14→Surface pending learning suggestions as yellow-tier governance feed cards with evidence display, confidence indicator, and Approve/Edit & Approve/Dismiss controls. Include a "Learnings" filter in the feed and provide a learning library view for curation of all learnings.
    15→
    16→## Domain Examples
    17→
    18→### 1: Happy Path -- Tomas approves a high-confidence suggestion
    19→Tomas opens the governance feed and sees a yellow card: "Never use null for domain data values," suggested by Observer with 89% confidence, targeting code_agent, with evidence from 3 sessions. He clicks "Approve." The learning transitions to "active" immediately, a confirmation appears, and the card disappears from the pending feed.
    20→
    21→### 2: Edge Case -- Tomas edits and approves a suggestion
    22→A pending suggestion reads "Avoid null in code." Tomas clicks "Edit & Approve," expands the text to "Never use null for domain data values. Represent absence with omitted optional fields (field?: Type) only. If null appears, treat as contract violation and fix the producer." He also adds chat_agent to the target agents. The refined learning is saved as active.
    23→
    24→### 3: Error/Boundary -- Tomas dismisses a low-confidence suggestion
    25→A suggestion "Prefer shorter task titles under 60 characters" has 62% confidence, suggested by PM agent. Tomas clicks "Dismiss," enters optional reason "too subjective, not a universal rule," and confirms. The learning is marked "dismissed" and removed from the feed. The dismissal reason is stored for future reference.
    26→
    27→## UAT Scenarios (BDD)
    28→
    29→### Scenario: Pending learning appears as yellow-tier feed card
    30→Given the Observer created a learning suggestion with status "pending_approval"
    31→And confidence is 0.89 and target_agents is ["code_agent"]
    32→When Tomas opens the governance feed
    33→Then a yellow-tier card appears showing the suggestion text
    34→And the card displays "Suggested by: Observer | Confidence: 89%"
    35→And the card displays "For: code_agent"
    36→And the card shows evidence from 3 linked sessions
    37→And buttons "Approve", "Edit & Approve", "Dismiss" are visible
    38→
    39→### Scenario: Approve transitions learning to active
    40→Given a pending learning suggestion is displayed in the feed
    41→When Tomas clicks "Approve"
    42→Then the learning status changes to "active"
    43→And approved_by references Tomas Eriksson
    44→And approved_at is set to current time
    45→And a confirmation "Learning approved and activated" appears
    46→And the card is removed from the pending feed
    47→
    48→### Scenario: Edit and approve with text refinement
    49→Given a pending learning suggestion is displayed
    50→When Tomas clicks "Edit & Approve"
    51→Then the learning editor opens with pre-filled text and settings
    52→When Tomas modifies the text and adds chat_agent to targets
    53→And clicks "Save & Activate"
    54→Then the learning is saved with edited text and status "active"
    55→
    56→### Scenario: Dismiss with reason
    57→Given a pending suggestion with confidence 0.62 is displayed
    58→When Tomas clicks "Dismiss"
    59→Then an optional reason input appears
    60→When Tomas enters "too subjective" and confirms
    61→Then the learning status changes to "dismissed"
    62→And dismissed_reason, dismissed_by, dismissed_at are recorded
    63→And the card is removed from the feed
    64→
    65→### Scenario: Feed filter for learnings
    66→Given 3 pending learning suggestions and 5 pending entity suggestions exist
    67→When Tomas selects the "Learnings" filter on the governance feed
    68→Then only the 3 learning suggestion cards are displayed
    69→
    70→### Scenario: Evidence drill-down
    71→Given a pending learning with 3 evidence_refs is displayed
    72→When Tomas expands the evidence section
    73→Then each evidence shows session date, correction quote, and session link
    74→
    75→### Scenario: Discuss action opens scoped chat
    76→Given a pending learning "Prefer connection pooling over single connections" is displayed
    77→When Tomas clicks "Discuss"
    78→Then a chat window opens scoped to the learning context
    79→And the chat agent has the learning text, evidence, and suggesting agent's rationale in context
    80→And Tomas can ask questions like "Why did the observer suggest this?" or "Show me the sessions where this pattern appeared"
    81→
    82→### Scenario: Deactivate an active learning from library
    83→Given Tomas views the learning library with 8 active learnings
    84→When he clicks "Deactivate" on the learning "Billing uses integer cents"
    85→Then a confirmation prompt appears "Deactivate this learning? It will no longer be injected into agent prompts."
    86→When Tomas confirms
    87→Then the learning status changes to "deactivated"
    88→And deactivated_at and deactivated_by are recorded
    89→And the learning is no longer returned by prompt injection queries
    90→And the learning remains visible in the library with "deactivated" badge
    91→
    92→### Scenario: Learning library view with filters
    93→Given workspace "Brain Development" has 8 active, 2 superseded, and 1 dismissed learning
    94→When Tomas navigates to the learning library
    95→Then all 11 learnings are listed with status badges
    96→And filter controls are available for: status, agent type, learning type
    97→When Tomas filters by status "active"
    98→Then only the 8 active learnings are shown
    99→And each card displays text, type, source, target agents, and created date
   100→
   101→### Scenario: Learning library grouped by target agent
   102→Given Tomas has active learnings across three agents:
   103→  | target_agent | text                                     | type        |
   104→  | architect    | Never recommend Redis                    | constraint  |
   105→  | architect    | Always consider horizontal scaling       | instruction |
   106→  | code_agent   | Use native fetch, never import axios     | constraint  |
   107→  | pm_agent     | Estimate 2x time for design review tasks | instruction |
   108→When Tomas views the learning library
   109→Then learnings are grouped by target agent with count per agent
   110→And each learning shows text, type badge, creation date, and source (human or agent name)
   111→
   112→### Scenario: Edit learning text from library
   113→Given an active learning exists for the Architect agent:
   114→  | text                                          | type        |
   115→  | Always recommend Valkey for caching            | constraint  |
   116→When Tomas edits the learning text to "Always recommend Valkey for caching in production. Redis is acceptable for local development."
   117→Then the learning text is updated in the database
   118→And the learning embedding is recomputed
   119→And the updated text is injected in the next Architect session
   120→
   121→### Scenario: Change learning type from library
   122→Given an active learning exists with type "precedent":
   123→  | text                                     | type      |
   124→  | We generally prefer PostgreSQL           | precedent |
   125→When Tomas changes the type to "constraint"
   126→Then the learning type is updated to "constraint"
   127→And the learning is now included in mandatory injection (constraints always included, even over token budget)
   128→
   129→### Scenario: Empty library for agent with no learnings
   130→Given no learnings exist for the Observer agent
   131→When Tomas views the Observer agent's learning library section
   132→Then an empty state is displayed: "No learnings for the Observer agent. Create one from a chat conversation."
   133→
   134→## Acceptance Criteria
   135→- [ ] Pending learnings (status = "pending_approval") appear as yellow-tier governance feed cards
   136→- [ ] Feed cards display: suggestion text, suggested_by, confidence, target_agents, evidence quotes
   137→- [ ] "Approve" transitions status to "active" with approved_by and approved_at
   138→- [ ] "Edit & Approve" opens learning editor pre-filled with suggestion data, saves as active on confirm
   139→- [ ] "Dismiss" captures optional reason and transitions to "dismissed" with dismissed_by and dismissed_at
   140→- [ ] Governance feed has a "Learnings" filter tab
   141→- [ ] Evidence drill-down shows session dates, correction quotes, and links to session records
   142→- [ ] Learning library view accessible from governance feed and navigation
   143→- [ ] Library shows all learnings with filters: status (active/pending/superseded/deactivated/dismissed), agent type, learning type
   144→- [ ] Each learning card shows text, type, source, status, target agents, created date
   145→- [ ] Deactivation changes status to "deactivated" with confirmation prompt, records deactivated_by and deactivated_at
   146→- [ ] Deactivated learnings remain visible in library but are not injected into prompts
   147→- [ ] Learning library grouped by target_agent with count per agent
   148→- [ ] Edit action allows modifying text and type fields
   149→- [ ] Edit triggers embedding recomputation
   150→- [ ] Empty state shown for agents with no active learnings
   151→- [ ] "Discuss" action on feed cards opens scoped chat with learning context pre-loaded
   152→
   153→## Technical Notes
   154→- Feed card rendering follows existing suggestion card pattern in `feed-queries.ts` and `feed-route.ts`
   155→- Learning status transitions follow the same actor-timestamped pattern as suggestion accept/dismiss
   156→- Feed query joins learning table WHERE status = "pending_approval" AND workspace = $workspace
   157→- Evidence rendering requires joining evidence_refs to agent_session records for date and correction text
   158→- Learning editor component is reused from US-AL-001 (human creation) with minor adaptation for pre-fill
   159→
   160→## Dependencies
   161→- Depends on: US-AL-005 (Learning Schema) -- table must exist
   162→- Depends on: US-AL-002 (Agent Suggests) -- suggestions must exist to display
   163→- Depends on: Existing feed infrastructure (feed-queries.ts, feed-route.ts)
   164→- Enables: US-AL-003 (Runtime Injection) -- approved learnings become injectable
   165→

# US-AL-004: Learning Governance Feed Cards

## Problem
Tomas Eriksson's workspace has accumulated 3 pending learning suggestions from the Observer and PM agents this week. He has no way to see them -- they exist in the database but do not surface in his governance feed. Without visibility, agent-suggested learnings pile up unreviewed, and the agents never benefit from the wisdom they detected.

## Who
- Workspace owner | Reviewing governance feed daily | Wants to efficiently approve, edit, or dismiss pending learnings

## Job Story Trace
- **Job 4**: Learning Governance and Curation
- **When** agents have suggested behavioral learnings, **I want to** review them in my governance feed with evidence, **so I can** approve valuable rules and dismiss low-quality ones efficiently.

## Solution
Surface pending learning suggestions as yellow-tier governance feed cards with evidence display, confidence indicator, and Approve/Edit & Approve/Dismiss controls. Include a "Learnings" filter in the feed and provide a learning library view for curation of all learnings.

## Domain Examples

### 1: Happy Path -- Tomas approves a high-confidence suggestion
Tomas opens the governance feed and sees a yellow card: "Never use null for domain data values," suggested by Observer with 89% confidence, targeting code_agent, with evidence from 3 sessions. He clicks "Approve." The learning transitions to "active" immediately, a confirmation appears, and the card disappears from the pending feed.

### 2: Edge Case -- Tomas edits and approves a suggestion
A pending suggestion reads "Avoid null in code." Tomas clicks "Edit & Approve," expands the text to "Never use null for domain data values. Represent absence with omitted optional fields (field?: Type) only. If null appears, treat as contract violation and fix the producer." He also adds chat_agent to the target agents. The refined learning is saved as active.

### 3: Error/Boundary -- Tomas dismisses a low-confidence suggestion
A suggestion "Prefer shorter task titles under 60 characters" has 62% confidence, suggested by PM agent. Tomas clicks "Dismiss," enters optional reason "too subjective, not a universal rule," and confirms. The learning is marked "dismissed" and removed from the feed. The dismissal reason is stored for future reference.

## UAT Scenarios (BDD)

### Scenario: Pending learning appears as yellow-tier feed card
Given the Observer created a learning suggestion with status "pending_approval"
And confidence is 0.89 and target_agents is ["code_agent"]
When Tomas opens the governance feed
Then a yellow-tier card appears showing the suggestion text
And the card displays "Suggested by: Observer | Confidence: 89%"
And the card displays "For: code_agent"
And the card shows evidence from 3 linked sessions
And buttons "Approve", "Edit & Approve", "Dismiss" are visible

### Scenario: Approve transitions learning to active
Given a pending learning suggestion is displayed in the feed
When Tomas clicks "Approve"
Then the learning status changes to "active"
And approved_by references Tomas Eriksson
And approved_at is set to current time
And a confirmation "Learning approved and activated" appears
And the card is removed from the pending feed

### Scenario: Edit and approve with text refinement
Given a pending learning suggestion is displayed
When Tomas clicks "Edit & Approve"
Then the learning editor opens with pre-filled text and settings
When Tomas modifies the text and adds chat_agent to targets
And clicks "Save & Activate"
Then the learning is saved with edited text and status "active"

### Scenario: Dismiss with reason
Given a pending suggestion with confidence 0.62 is displayed
When Tomas clicks "Dismiss"
Then an optional reason input appears
When Tomas enters "too subjective" and confirms
Then the learning status changes to "dismissed"
And dismissed_reason, dismissed_by, dismissed_at are recorded
And the card is removed from the feed

### Scenario: Feed filter for learnings
Given 3 pending learning suggestions and 5 pending entity suggestions exist
When Tomas selects the "Learnings" filter on the governance feed
Then only the 3 learning suggestion cards are displayed

### Scenario: Evidence drill-down
Given a pending learning with 3 evidence_refs is displayed
When Tomas expands the evidence section
Then each evidence shows session date, correction quote, and session link

### Scenario: Discuss action opens scoped chat
Given a pending learning "Prefer connection pooling over single connections" is displayed
When Tomas clicks "Discuss"
Then a chat window opens scoped to the learning context
And the chat agent has the learning text, evidence, and suggesting agent's rationale in context
And Tomas can ask questions like "Why did the observer suggest this?" or "Show me the sessions where this pattern appeared"

### Scenario: Deactivate an active learning from library
Given Tomas views the learning library with 8 active learnings
When he clicks "Deactivate" on the learning "Billing uses integer cents"
Then a confirmation prompt appears "Deactivate this learning? It will no longer be injected into agent prompts."
When Tomas confirms
Then the learning status changes to "deactivated"
And deactivated_at and deactivated_by are recorded
And the learning is no longer returned by prompt injection queries
And the learning remains visible in the library with "deactivated" badge

### Scenario: Learning library view with filters
Given workspace "Brain Development" has 8 active, 2 superseded, and 1 dismissed learning
When Tomas navigates to the learning library
Then all 11 learnings are listed with status badges
And filter controls are available for: status, agent type, learning type
When Tomas filters by status "active"
Then only the 8 active learnings are shown
And each card displays text, type, source, target agents, and created date

### Scenario: Learning library grouped by target agent
Given Tomas has active learnings across three agents:
  | target_agent | text                                     | type        |
  | architect    | Never recommend Redis                    | constraint  |
  | architect    | Always consider horizontal scaling       | instruction |
  | code_agent   | Use native fetch, never import axios     | constraint  |
  | pm_agent     | Estimate 2x time for design review tasks | instruction |
When Tomas views the learning library
Then learnings are grouped by target agent with count per agent
And each learning shows text, type badge, creation date, and source (human or agent name)

### Scenario: Edit learning text from library
Given an active learning exists for the Architect agent:
  | text                                          | type        |
  | Always recommend Valkey for caching            | constraint  |
When Tomas edits the learning text to "Always recommend Valkey for caching in production. Redis is acceptable for local development."
Then the learning text is updated in the database
And the learning embedding is recomputed
And the updated text is injected in the next Architect session

### Scenario: Change learning type from library
Given an active learning exists with type "precedent":
  | text                                     | type      |
  | We generally prefer PostgreSQL           | precedent |
When Tomas changes the type to "constraint"
Then the learning type is updated to "constraint"
And the learning is now included in mandatory injection (constraints always included, even over token budget)

### Scenario: Empty library for agent with no learnings
Given no learnings exist for the Observer agent
When Tomas views the Observer agent's learning library section
Then an empty state is displayed: "No learnings for the Observer agent. Create one from a chat conversation."

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Pending learnings (status = "pending_approval") appear as yellow-tier governance feed cards
- [ ] Feed cards display: suggestion text, suggested_by, confidence, target_agents, evidence quotes
- [ ] "Approve" transitions status to "active" with approved_by and approved_at
- [ ] "Edit & Approve" opens learning editor pre-filled with suggestion data, saves as active on confirm
- [ ] "Dismiss" captures optional reason and transitions to "dismissed" with dismissed_by and dismissed_at
- [ ] Governance feed has a "Learnings" filter tab
- [ ] Evidence drill-down shows session dates, correction quotes, and links to session records
- [ ] Learning library view accessible from governance feed and navigation
- [ ] Library shows all learnings with filters: status (active/pending/superseded/deactivated/dismissed), agent type, learning type
- [ ] Each learning card shows text, type, source, status, target agents, created date
- [ ] Deactivation changes status to "deactivated" with confirmation prompt, records deactivated_by and deactivated_at
- [ ] Deactivated learnings remain visible in library but are not injected into prompts
- [ ] Learning library grouped by target_agent with count per agent
- [ ] Edit action allows modifying text and type fields
- [ ] Edit triggers embedding recomputation
- [ ] Empty state shown for agents with no active learnings
- [ ] "Discuss" action on feed cards opens scoped chat with learning context pre-loaded

## Technical Notes
- Feed card rendering follows existing suggestion card pattern in `feed-queries.ts` and `feed-route.ts`
- Learning status transitions follow the same actor-timestamped pattern as suggestion accept/dismiss
- Feed query joins learning table WHERE status = "pending_approval" AND workspace = $workspace
- Evidence rendering requires joining evidence_refs to agent_session records for date and correction text
- Learning editor component is reused from US-AL-001 (human creation) with minor adaptation for pre-fill

## Dependencies
- Depends on: US-AL-005 (Learning Schema) -- table must exist
- Depends on: US-AL-002 (Agent Suggests) -- suggestions must exist to display
- Depends on: Existing feed infrastructure (feed-queries.ts, feed-route.ts)
- Enables: US-AL-003 (Runtime Injection) -- approved learnings become injectable

input.prompt:
# DESIGN Wave: Architecture Design for Agent Learnings Feature

Execute *design-architecture for the "agent-learnings" feature in the Brain knowledge graph platform.

## Project Context

Brain is the operating system for autonomous organizations — a self-correcting knowledge graph that gives AI agents shared memory, governed autonomy, and verifiable intent. It's built with:
- **Backend**: Bun (Bun.serve) + TypeScript
- **Database**: SurrealDB (graph + vector + document)
- **Frontend**: React + Tiptap + Reagraph
- **LLM**: Provider-agnostic (OpenRouter/Ollama)
- **Auth**: Better Auth + OAuth 2.1 + RAR + DPoP
- **Development Paradigm**: Functional (already set in CLAUDE.md)

## Feature Summary

Agent Learnings is the "Synaptic Plasticity" of Brain — persistent behavioral modifications that convert short-term agent failures into long-term wisdom. Two sources: human-created (immediately active) and agent-suggested (pending human approval). Injected into agent system prompts at runtime (JIT prompting).

## JTBD Analysis (4 Jobs)

**Job 1: Persistent Agent Correction** (Score: 18.0 - Extremely Underserved)
- When: corrected same agent mistake 3rd time this week
- Want: record a permanent behavioral rule
- So: stop wasting time repeating corrections

**Job 2: Agent Self-Improvement** (Score: 14.0 - Underserved)  
- When: agent notices same pattern corrected 3+ times
- Want: suggest a behavioral learning for human approval
- So: prevent future failures without requiring human initiative

**Job 3: Runtime Learning Consumption** (Score: 14.5 - Extremely Underserved)
- When: agent starts new session with accumulated learnings
- Want: right learnings injected into system prompt
- So: operate with accumulated wisdom while keeping context efficient

**Job 4: Learning Governance and Curation** (Score: 12.5 - Underserved)
- When: workspace has 30+ learnings, some outdated/conflicting
- Want: review, curate, resolve conflicts
- So: maintain coherent behavioral ruleset

## User Stories (6 stories, all DoR PASSED)

**US-AL-005: Learning Entity Schema** (Must Have, 1-2 days)
- SCHEMAFULL `learning` table with text, learning_type (constraint|instruction|precedent), status (active|pending_approval|dismissed|superseded|deactivated), source (human|agent), target_agents array, workspace, embedding, created_by, timestamps
- Relation tables: `learning_evidence` TYPE RELATION (polymorphic OUT: message|trace|observation|agent_session), `supersedes` TYPE RELATION
- HNSW index on embedding (1536 dim, COSINE), workspace+status index
- Status lifecycle: human→active immediately; agent→pending_approval→active|dismissed; any active→superseded|deactivated

**US-AL-001: Human Creates Persistent Learning** (Must Have, 2-3 days)
- Chat agent detects repeated correction patterns (3+ same topic in 14 days)
- Offers inline "Save as Learning" card
- Learning editor pre-fills from correction context (text, type, agent scope)
- Human-created learnings immediately active (no approval)
- Duplicate detection (similarity > 0.85), conflict detection before save
- Supersession changes old learning status

**US-AL-003: JIT Learning Injection** (Must Have, 2 days)
- Shared function `loadActiveLearnings(surreal, workspaceRecord, agentType)` 
- Priority sort: human > agent, then by priority (high>med>low), then created_at
- Type-based injection: constraints ALWAYS injected, instructions within budget, precedents only when semantically relevant (similarity > 0.70)
- Token budget ~500 tokens, constraints never dropped
- Inject into 4 prompt builders: chat agent, PM agent, MCP context, Observer context
- Format as "Workspace Learnings" section grouped by type

**US-AL-004: Governance Feed Cards** (Should Have, 2-3 days)
- Pending learnings as yellow-tier feed cards with evidence, confidence, actions
- Approve/Edit & Approve/Dismiss flows
- Learning library view with filters (status, agent type, learning type)
- Deactivation, edit, and "Discuss" actions

**US-AL-002: Agent Suggests Learning** (Should Have, 2-3 days)
- Three data sources for detection:
  1. Conversation history (chat agent detects corrections in real-time via LLM)
  2. Trace table (observer scans for repeated tool failures)
  3. Observation escalation (observer clusters observations by embedding similarity)
- Suggestions with status pending_approval, suggested_by, confidence, evidence_refs
- Rate limiting: max 5 per agent per workspace per week
- Re-suggestion prevention for dismissed learnings (similarity > 0.85)

**US-AL-006: Collision Detection** (Should Have, 2-3 days)
- Three-layer collision check before activation: active learnings (sim > 0.75), policies, decisions (sim > 0.80)
- Collision types: contradicts, duplicates (>0.90), overlaps, reinforces
- Policy collision = hard block; learning collision = warning with options; decision = informational
- Priority weighting: policy > learning, human > agent, constraint > instruction > precedent
- LLM intent analysis for contradiction classification
- Fail-open for human-created when embedding service unavailable

## Existing Codebase Integration Points

The handoff document identifies these integration points:
| Integration Point | File | What Changes |
|---|---|---|
| Schema | `schema/migrations/NNNN_add_learning_table.surql` | New migration |
| Chat prompt | `app/src/server/chat/context.ts` | Add learnings to ChatContext, inject in buildSystemPrompt |
| PM prompt | `app/src/server/agents/pm/prompt.ts` | Add learnings section |
| MCP context | `cli/src/context-builder.ts` | Add learnings to context packet |
| Observer context | `app/src/server/agents/observer/context-loader.ts` | Add learnings section |
| Feed queries | `app/src/server/feed/feed-queries.ts` | Add learning feed items |
| Feed route | `app/src/server/feed/feed-route.ts` | Add learning endpoints |
| Shared contracts | `app/src/shared/contracts.ts` | Add LearningSummary type |
| Server routes | `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` | Register learning routes |

## Key Technical Constraints (from CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md)

1. **No null**: Use `undefined` via optional properties
2. **SCHEMAFULL only**: All tables SCHEMAFULL, every nested key declared
3. **KNN + WHERE bug**: Must use two-step pattern (KNN subquery, then filter)
4. **RecordId objects**: Never raw `table:id` strings in internal logic
5. **No module-level singletons**: Pass shared state via DI
6. **Fail fast**: Throw on invalid state, no silent fallbacks
7. **No backwards compatibility**: Schema changes are breaking, no data migrations
8. **Functional paradigm**: Pure functions, pipeline composition, types as documentation
9. **`--no-verify` for git**: Pre-commit hook requires brain init not available in worktrees
10. **BM25 full-text search**: For UI entity search; vector for semantic similarity
11. **Fire-and-forget tracking**: Use `deps.inflight.track()` for background DB ops
12. **`DEFINE EVENT ... ASYNC`**: For webhook-style side effects

## Shared Artifacts Registry

10 tracked artifacts with sources, consumers, risk levels:
- learning_text (HIGH risk - 7 consumers)
- learning_type (MEDIUM risk)
- learning_status (HIGH risk - controls injection)
- target_agents (HIGH risk - controls which agents receive)
- learning_source (MEDIUM risk)
- suggested_by, evidence_refs, pattern_confidence (LOW-MEDIUM risk)
- workspace_record, agent_type (HIGH risk - existing)

## Architecture Design Decisions Needed

From handoff document:
1. Learning table design: New table (recommended) vs extending suggestion table
2. Correction detection: 3 data sources defined (conversation, trace, observation)
3. Token budget strategy: Hard vs soft limit, exact tokenizer vs word-count heuristic
4. Conflict detection depth: Embedding similarity alone vs embedding + LLM intent
5. Observer integration: New scan types in existing Observer loop
6. Feed card component: Extend existing suggestion card vs new component

## Implementation Order

```
Phase 1: US-AL-005 (Schema) — foundation
Phase 2: US-AL-001 (Human Creates) + US-AL-003 (Injection) — parallel, core value
Phase 3: US-AL-004 (Feed Cards) — governance
Phase 4: US-AL-002 (Agent Suggests) — self-improvement  
Phase 5: US-AL-006 (Collision Detection) — safety net
```

## Your Task

1. **Analyze the existing codebase** to understand current patterns for entities (observation, suggestion), feed cards, prompt builders, and MCP context
2. **Design the architecture** for agent-learnings following the functional paradigm
3. **Produce C4 diagrams** (System Context + Container, mandatory in Mermaid)
4. **Write ADRs** for significant decisions
5. **Define component boundaries** with dependency-inversion compliance
6. **Select technology stack** choices with rationale
7. **Create data models** aligned with SurrealDB schema patterns

Output files should go to:
```
docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/
  architecture-design.md
  technology-stack.md  
  component-boundaries.md
  data-models.md
docs/adrs/
  ADR-NNN-*.md
```

The architecture design is complete. Here is a summary of what was produced and where all files are located.

---

## Architecture Design Complete - Agent Learnings

### Deliverables

**Architecture Design Document**
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/architecture-design.md`
  - C4 System Context (L1), Container (L2), Component (L3) diagrams in Mermaid
  - Component architecture with 5 domain components + 1 HTTP adapter
  - Integration patterns: JIT injection, collision detection, pattern detection
  - Quality attribute strategies (performance, security, reliability, maintainability, testability)
  - Implementation roadmap: 7 steps across 5 phases
  - Self-review against critique dimensions
  - Quality gate checklist (all passing)

**Supporting Documents**
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/data-models.md` - SurrealDB schema (learning table, learning_evidence relation, supersedes relation), status lifecycle, query patterns with two-step KNN, shared contract types
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/component-boundaries.md` - Dependency map, component responsibilities, dependency-inversion compliance, integration boundary contracts for all 4 prompt builders + feed
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/technology-stack.md` - Reuse analysis confirming zero new dependencies, technology selection rationale

**ADRs**
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-026-word-count-heuristic-for-learning-token-budget.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-027-dedicated-learning-table-over-suggestion-extension.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-028-three-layer-collision-detection-for-learnings.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-029-workspace-scoped-learnings-not-project-scoped.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-030-jit-learning-injection-at-session-start.md`

### Key Architecture Decisions

1. **Dedicated `learning` table** (not extending suggestion) -- different lifecycle, schema, and consumption pattern justify separation
2. **Workspace-scoped, not project-scoped** -- learnings are cross-cutting behavioral rules; `target_agents` provides sufficient scoping
3. **JIT injection at session start** -- one DB query per session, not per message; new learnings picked up on next conversation
4. **Word-count heuristic** for ~500 token budget -- zero-dependency, sufficient precision for soft budget
5. **Three-layer collision detection** -- learning/policy/decision with escalating severity; LLM only for ambiguous similarity zone

### New Production Files (8 files)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `schema/migrations/0030_learning_table.surql` | Schema migration |
| `app/src/server/learning/queries.ts` | CRUD + status transitions |
| `app/src/server/learning/loader.ts` | JIT loading with priority sort + token budget |
| `app/src/server/learning/formatter.ts` | Pure prompt section renderer |
| `app/src/server/learning/collision.ts` | Three-layer collision detection |
| `app/src/server/learning/detector.ts` | Pattern detection (3 sources) |
| `app/src/server/learning/learning-route.ts` | HTTP endpoints |
| `app/src/server/learning/types.ts` | Internal type definitions |

### Existing Files Modified (additive only)

`shared/contracts.ts`, `chat/context.ts`, `agents/pm/prompt.ts`, `agents/observer/prompt.ts`, `mcp/context-builder.ts`, `feed/feed-queries.ts`, `feed/feed-route.ts`, `runtime/start-server.ts`

### Handoff to Acceptance Designer

This architecture is ready for the DISTILL wave. The acceptance-designer should use the implementation roadmap (7 steps, 5 phases) and component boundaries to define acceptance tests. The functional paradigm is set -- pure core (`formatter.ts`, `loader.ts` sorting logic) with effect boundaries at the adapter layer (`queries.ts`, `collision.ts` LLM calls, `learning-route.ts`).

4. Conflict detection depth: Embedding similarity alone vs embedding + LLM intent
5. Observer integration: New scan types in existing Observer loop
6. Feed card component: Extend existing suggestion card vs new component

## Implementation Order

```
Phase 1: US-AL-005 (Schema) — foundation
Phase 2: US-AL-001 (Human Creates) + US-AL-003 (Injection) — parallel, core value
Phase 3: US-AL-004 (Feed Cards) — governance
Phase 4: US-AL-002 (Agent Suggests) — self-improvement  
Phase 5: US-AL-006 (Collision Detection) — safety net
```

## Your Task

1. **Analyze the existing codebase** to understand current patterns for entities (observation, suggestion), feed cards, prompt builders, and MCP context
2. **Design the architecture** for agent-learnings following the functional paradigm
3. **Produce C4 diagrams** (System Context + Container, mandatory in Mermaid)
4. **Write ADRs** for significant decisions
5. **Define component boundaries** with dependency-inversion compliance
6. **Select technology stack** choices with rationale
7. **Create data models** aligned with SurrealDB schema patterns

Output files should go to:
```
docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/
  architecture-design.md
  technology-stack.md  
  component-boundaries.md
  data-models.md
docs/adrs/
  ADR-NNN-*.md
```

The architecture design is complete. Here is a summary of what was produced and where all files are located.

---

## Architecture Design Complete - Agent Learnings

### Deliverables

**Architecture Design Document**
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/architecture-design.md`
  - C4 System Context (L1), Container (L2), Component (L3) diagrams in Mermaid
  - Component architecture with 5 domain components + 1 HTTP adapter
  - Integration patterns: JIT injection, collision detection, pattern detection
  - Quality attribute strategies (performance, security, reliability, maintainability, testability)
  - Implementation roadmap: 7 steps across 5 phases
  - Self-review against critique dimensions
  - Quality gate checklist (all passing)

**Supporting Documents**
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/data-models.md` - SurrealDB schema (learning table, learning_evidence relation, supersedes relation), status lifecycle, query patterns with two-step KNN, shared contract types
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/component-boundaries.md` - Dependency map, component responsibilities, dependency-inversion compliance, integration boundary contracts for all 4 prompt builders + feed
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/technology-stack.md` - Reuse analysis confirming zero new dependencies, technology selection rationale

**ADRs**
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-026-word-count-heuristic-for-learning-token-budget.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-027-dedicated-learning-table-over-suggestion-extension.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-028-three-layer-collision-detection-for-learnings.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-029-workspace-scoped-learnings-not-project-scoped.md`
- `/Users/marcus/conductor/workspaces/brain-v1/karachi/docs/adrs/ADR-030-jit-learning-injection-at-session-start.md`

### Key Architecture Decisions

1. **Dedicated `learning` table** (not extending suggestion) -- different lifecycle, schema, and consumption pattern justify separation
2. **Workspace-scoped, not project-scoped** -- learnings are cross-cutting behavioral rules; `target_agents` provides sufficient scoping
3. **JIT injection at session start** -- one DB query per session, not per message; new learnings picked up on next conversation
4. **Word-count heuristic** for ~500 token budget -- zero-dependency, sufficient precision for soft budget
5. **Three-layer collision detection** -- learning/policy/decision with escalating severity; LLM only for ambiguous similarity zone

### New Production Files (8 files)

| File | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `schema/migrations/0030_learning_table.surql` | Schema migration |
| `app/src/server/learning/queries.ts` | CRUD + status transitions |
| `app/src/server/learning/loader.ts` | JIT loading with priority sort + token budget |
| `app/src/server/learning/formatter.ts` | Pure prompt section renderer |
| `app/src/server/learning/collision.ts` | Three-layer collision detection |
| `app/src/server/learning/detector.ts` | Pattern detection (3 sources) |
| `app/src/server/learning/learning-route.ts` | HTTP endpoints |
| `app/src/server/learning/types.ts` | Internal type definitions |

### Existing Files Modified (additive only)

`shared/contracts.ts`, `chat/context.ts`, `agents/pm/prompt.ts`, `agents/observer/prompt.ts`, `mcp/context-builder.ts`, `feed/feed-queries.ts`, `feed/feed-route.ts`, `runtime/start-server.ts`

### Handoff to Acceptance Designer

This architecture is ready for the DISTILL wave. The acceptance-designer should use the implementation roadmap (7 steps, 5 phases) and component boundaries to define acceptance tests. The functional paradigm is set -- pure core (`formatter.ts`, `loader.ts` sorting logic) with effect boundaries at the adapter layer (`queries.ts`, `collision.ts` LLM calls, `learning-route.ts`).

Architecture design is complete. Here's what was produced:

### Design Documents
- `docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/architecture-design.md` — C4 diagrams (L1-L3 in Mermaid), component architecture, quality attributes, implementation roadmap
- `docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/data-models.md` — SurrealDB schema, status lifecycle, query patterns
- `docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/component-boundaries.md` — Dependency map, DI compliance, integration contracts
- `docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/technology-stack.md` — Zero new dependencies, reuse rationale

### ADRs
- **ADR-026**: Word-count heuristic for token budget
- **ADR-027**: Dedicated learning table (not extending suggestion)
- **ADR-028**: Three-layer collision detection
- **ADR-029**: Workspace-scoped learnings (not project-scoped)
- **ADR-030**: JIT injection at session start

### Key Decisions
1. **Dedicated `learning` table** — different lifecycle justifies separation from suggestion
2. **Workspace-scoped** — `target_agents` provides sufficient scoping without project complexity
3. **JIT injection** — one DB query per session, not per message
4. **Word-count heuristic** — zero-dependency, sufficient for soft ~500 token budget
5. **Three-layer collision** — learning/policy/decision with escalating severity; LLM only for ambiguous cases

### 8 new production files planned in `app/src/server/learning/`:
`queries.ts`, `loader.ts`, `formatter.ts`, `collision.ts`, `detector.ts`, `learning-route.ts`, `types.ts` + migration

Ready for DISTILL wave (acceptance test design) or review. Want me to proceed with either?

content.text:
# DESIGN Wave: Architecture Design for Agent Learnings Feature

Execute *design-architecture for the "agent-learnings" feature in the Brain knowledge graph platform.

## Project Context

Brain is the operating system for autonomous organizations — a self-correcting knowledge graph that gives AI agents shared memory, governed autonomy, and verifiable intent. It's built with:
- **Backend**: Bun (Bun.serve) + TypeScript
- **Database**: SurrealDB (graph + vector + document)
- **Frontend**: React + Tiptap + Reagraph
- **LLM**: Provider-agnostic (OpenRouter/Ollama)
- **Auth**: Better Auth + OAuth 2.1 + RAR + DPoP
- **Development Paradigm**: Functional (already set in CLAUDE.md)

## Feature Summary

Agent Learnings is the "Synaptic Plasticity" of Brain — persistent behavioral modifications that convert short-term agent failures into long-term wisdom. Two sources: human-created (immediately active) and agent-suggested (pending human approval). Injected into agent system prompts at runtime (JIT prompting).

## JTBD Analysis (4 Jobs)

**Job 1: Persistent Agent Correction** (Score: 18.0 - Extremely Underserved)
- When: corrected same agent mistake 3rd time this week
- Want: record a permanent behavioral rule
- So: stop wasting time repeating corrections

**Job 2: Agent Self-Improvement** (Score: 14.0 - Underserved)  
- When: agent notices same pattern corrected 3+ times
- Want: suggest a behavioral learning for human approval
- So: prevent future failures without requiring human initiative

**Job 3: Runtime Learning Consumption** (Score: 14.5 - Extremely Underserved)
- When: agent starts new session with accumulated learnings
- Want: right learnings injected into system prompt
- So: operate with accumulated wisdom while keeping context efficient

**Job 4: Learning Governance and Curation** (Score: 12.5 - Underserved)
- When: workspace has 30+ learnings, some outdated/conflicting
- Want: review, curate, resolve conflicts
- So: maintain coherent behavioral ruleset

## User Stories (6 stories, all DoR PASSED)

**US-AL-005: Learning Entity Schema** (Must Have, 1-2 days)
- SCHEMAFULL `learning` table with text, learning_type (constraint|instruction|precedent), status (active|pending_approval|dismissed|superseded|deactivated), source (human|agent), target_agents array, workspace, embedding, created_by, timestamps
- Relation tables: `learning_evidence` TYPE RELATION (polymorphic OUT: message|trace|observation|agent_session), `supersedes` TYPE RELATION
- HNSW index on embedding (1536 dim, COSINE), workspace+status index
- Status lifecycle: human→active immediately; agent→pending_approval→active|dismissed; any active→superseded|deactivated

**US-AL-001: Human Creates Persistent Learning** (Must Have, 2-3 days)
- Chat agent detects repeated correction patterns (3+ same topic in 14 days)
- Offers inline "Save as Learning" card
- Learning editor pre-fills from correction context (text, type, agent scope)
- Human-created learnings immediately active (no approval)
- Duplicate detection (similarity > 0.85), conflict detection before save
- Supersession changes old learning status

**US-AL-003: JIT Learning Injection** (Must Have, 2 days)
- Shared function `loadActiveLearnings(surreal, workspaceRecord, agentType)` 
- Priority sort: human > agent, then by priority (high>med>low), then created_at
- Type-based injection: constraints ALWAYS injected, instructions within budget, precedents only when semantically relevant (similarity > 0.70)
- Token budget ~500 tokens, constraints never dropped
- Inject into 4 prompt builders: chat agent, PM agent, MCP context, Observer context
- Format as "Workspace Learnings" section grouped by type

**US-AL-004: Governance Feed Cards** (Should Have, 2-3 days)
- Pending learnings as yellow-tier feed cards with evidence, confidence, actions
- Approve/Edit & Approve/Dismiss flows
- Learning library view with filters (status, agent type, learning type)
- Deactivation, edit, and "Discuss" actions

**US-AL-002: Agent Suggests Learning** (Should Have, 2-3 days)
- Three data sources for detection:
  1. Conversation history (chat agent detects corrections in real-time via LLM)
  2. Trace table (observer scans for repeated tool failures)
  3. Observation escalation (observer clusters observations by embedding similarity)
- Suggestions with status pending_approval, suggested_by, confidence, evidence_refs
- Rate limiting: max 5 per agent per workspace per week
- Re-suggestion prevention for dismissed learnings (similarity > 0.85)

**US-AL-006: Collision Detection** (Should Have, 2-3 days)
- Three-layer collision check before activation: active learnings (sim > 0.75), policies, decisions (sim > 0.80)
- Collision types: contradicts, duplicates (>0.90), overlaps, reinforces
- Policy collision = hard block; learning collision = warning with options; decision = informational
- Priority weighting: policy > learning, human > agent, constraint > instruction > precedent
- LLM intent analysis for contradiction classification
- Fail-open for human-created when embedding service unavailable

## Existing Codebase Integration Points

The handoff document identifies these integration points:
| Integration Point | File | What Changes |
|---|---|---|
| Schema | `schema/migrations/NNNN_add_learning_table.surql` | New migration |
| Chat prompt | `app/src/server/chat/context.ts` | Add learnings to ChatContext, inject in buildSystemPrompt |
| PM prompt | `app/src/server/agents/pm/prompt.ts` | Add learnings section |
| MCP context | `cli/src/context-builder.ts` | Add learnings to context packet |
| Observer context | `app/src/server/agents/observer/context-loader.ts` | Add learnings section |
| Feed queries | `app/src/server/feed/feed-queries.ts` | Add learning feed items |
| Feed route | `app/src/server/feed/feed-route.ts` | Add learning endpoints |
| Shared contracts | `app/src/shared/contracts.ts` | Add LearningSummary type |
| Server routes | `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` | Register learning routes |

## Key Technical Constraints (from CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md)

1. **No null**: Use `undefined` via optional properties
2. **SCHEMAFULL only**: All tables SCHEMAFULL, every nested key declared
3. **KNN + WHERE bug**: Must use two-step pattern (KNN subquery, then filter)
4. **RecordId objects**: Never raw `table:id` strings in internal logic
5. **No module-level singletons**: Pass shared state via DI
6. **Fail fast**: Throw on invalid state, no silent fallbacks
7. **No backwards compatibility**: Schema changes are breaking, no data migrations
8. **Functional paradigm**: Pure functions, pipeline composition, types as documentation
9. **`--no-verify` for git**: Pre-commit hook requires brain init not available in worktrees
10. **BM25 full-text search**: For UI entity search; vector for semantic similarity
11. **Fire-and-forget tracking**: Use `deps.inflight.track()` for background DB ops
12. **`DEFINE EVENT ... ASYNC`**: For webhook-style side effects

## Shared Artifacts Registry

10 tracked artifacts with sources, consumers, risk levels:
- learning_text (HIGH risk - 7 consumers)
- learning_type (MEDIUM risk)
- learning_status (HIGH risk - controls injection)
- target_agents (HIGH risk - controls which agents receive)
- learning_source (MEDIUM risk)
- suggested_by, evidence_refs, pattern_confidence (LOW-MEDIUM risk)
- workspace_record, agent_type (HIGH risk - existing)

## Architecture Design Decisions Needed

From handoff document:
1. Learning table design: New table (recommended) vs extending suggestion table
2. Correction detection: 3 data sources defined (conversation, trace, observation)
3. Token budget strategy: Hard vs soft limit, exact tokenizer vs word-count heuristic
4. Conflict detection depth: Embedding similarity alone vs embedding + LLM intent
5. Observer integration: New scan types in existing Observer loop
6. Feed card component: Extend existing suggestion card vs new component

## Implementation Order

```
Phase 1: US-AL-005 (Schema) — foundation
Phase 2: US-AL-001 (Human Creates) + US-AL-003 (Injection) — parallel, core value
Phase 3: US-AL-004 (Feed Cards) — governance
Phase 4: US-AL-002 (Agent Suggests) — self-improvement  
Phase 5: US-AL-006 (Collision Detection) — safety net
```

## Your Task

1. **Analyze the existing codebase** to understand current patterns for entities (observation, suggestion), feed cards, prompt builders, and MCP context
2. **Design the architecture** for agent-learnings following the functional paradigm
3. **Produce C4 diagrams** (System Context + Container, mandatory in Mermaid)
4. **Write ADRs** for significant decisions
5. **Define component boundaries** with dependency-inversion compliance
6. **Select technology stack** choices with rationale
7. **Create data models** aligned with SurrealDB schema patterns

Output files should go to:
```
docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/
  architecture-design.md
  technology-stack.md  
  component-boundaries.md
  data-models.md
docs/adrs/
  ADR-NNN-*.md
```

  3. Observation escalation (observer clusters observations by embedding similarity)
- Suggestions with status pending_approval, suggested_by, confidence, evidence_refs
- Rate limiting: max 5 per agent per workspace per week
- Re-suggestion prevention for dismissed learnings (similarity > 0.85)

**US-AL-006: Collision Detection** (Should Have, 2-3 days)
- Three-layer collision check before activation: active learnings (sim > 0.75), policies, decisions (sim > 0.80)
- Collision types: contradicts, duplicates (>0.90), overlaps, reinforces
- Policy collision = hard block; learning collision = warning with options; decision = informational
- Priority weighting: policy > learning, human > agent, constraint > instruction > precedent
- LLM intent analysis for contradiction classification
- Fail-open for human-created when embedding service unavailable

## Existing Codebase Integration Points

The handoff document identifies these integration points:
| Integration Point | File | What Changes |
|---|---|---|
| Schema | `schema/migrations/NNNN_add_learning_table.surql` | New migration |
| Chat prompt | `app/src/server/chat/context.ts` | Add learnings to ChatContext, inject in buildSystemPrompt |
| PM prompt | `app/src/server/agents/pm/prompt.ts` | Add learnings section |
| MCP context | `cli/src/context-builder.ts` | Add learnings to context packet |
| Observer context | `app/src/server/agents/observer/context-loader.ts` | Add learnings section |
| Feed queries | `app/src/server/feed/feed-queries.ts` | Add learning feed items |
| Feed route | `app/src/server/feed/feed-route.ts` | Add learning endpoints |
| Shared contracts | `app/src/shared/contracts.ts` | Add LearningSummary type |
| Server routes | `app/src/server/runtime/start-server.ts` | Register learning routes |

## Key Technical Constraints (from CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md)

1. **No null**: Use `undefined` via optional properties
2. **SCHEMAFULL only**: All tables SCHEMAFULL, every nested key declared
3. **KNN + WHERE bug**: Must use two-step pattern (KNN subquery, then filter)
4. **RecordId objects**: Never raw `table:id` strings in internal logic
5. **No module-level singletons**: Pass shared state via DI
6. **Fail fast**: Throw on invalid state, no silent fallbacks
7. **No backwards compatibility**: Schema changes are breaking, no data migrations
8. **Functional paradigm**: Pure functions, pipeline composition, types as documentation
9. **`--no-verify` for git**: Pre-commit hook requires brain init not available in worktrees
10. **BM25 full-text search**: For UI entity search; vector for semantic similarity
11. **Fire-and-forget tracking**: Use `deps.inflight.track()` for background DB ops
12. **`DEFINE EVENT ... ASYNC`**: For webhook-style side effects

## Shared Artifacts Registry

10 tracked artifacts with sources, consumers, risk levels:
- learning_text (HIGH risk - 7 consumers)
- learning_type (MEDIUM risk)
- learning_status (HIGH risk - controls injection)
- target_agents (HIGH risk - controls which agents receive)
- learning_source (MEDIUM risk)
- suggested_by, evidence_refs, pattern_confidence (LOW-MEDIUM risk)
- workspace_record, agent_type (HIGH risk - existing)

## Architecture Design Decisions Needed

From handoff document:
1. Learning table design: New table (recommended) vs extending suggestion table
2. Correction detection: 3 data sources defined (conversation, trace, observation)
3. Token budget strategy: Hard vs soft limit, exact tokenizer vs word-count heuristic
4. Conflict detection depth: Embedding similarity alone vs embedding + LLM intent
5. Observer integration: New scan types in existing Observer loop
6. Feed card component: Extend existing suggestion card vs new component

## Implementation Order

```
Phase 1: US-AL-005 (Schema) — foundation
Phase 2: US-AL-001 (Human Creates) + US-AL-003 (Injection) — parallel, core value
Phase 3: US-AL-004 (Feed Cards) — governance
Phase 4: US-AL-002 (Agent Suggests) — self-improvement  
Phase 5: US-AL-006 (Collision Detection) — safety net
```

## Your Task

1. **Analyze the existing codebase** to understand current patterns for entities (observation, suggestion), feed cards, prompt builders, and MCP context
2. **Design the architecture** for agent-learnings following the functional paradigm
3. **Produce C4 diagrams** (System Context + Container, mandatory in Mermaid)
4. **Write ADRs** for significant decisions
5. **Define component boundaries** with dependency-inversion compliance
6. **Select technology stack** choices with rationale
7. **Create data models** aligned with SurrealDB schema patterns

Output files should go to:
```
docs/feature/agent-learnings/design/
  architecture-design.md
  technology-stack.md  
  component-boundaries.md
  data-models.md
docs/adrs/
  ADR-NNN-*.md
```

1→---
     2→name: architecture-patterns
     3→description: Comprehensive architecture patterns, methodologies, quality frameworks, and evaluation methods for solution architects. Load when designing system architecture or selecting patterns.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Architecture Patterns and Methodologies
     7→
     8→## C4 Model -- Hierarchical Architecture Visualization
     9→
    10→Four levels for different audiences:
    11→1. **System Context**: system + users + external systems (stakeholder view)
    12→2. **Containers**: applications, data stores, deployment units (technical overview)
    13→3. **Components**: internal modules within containers (developer view)
    14→4. **Code**: class/module level (optional, often auto-generated)
    15→
    16→Notation/tooling independent. Reduces communication overhead, shared visual language across stakeholders.
    17→
    18→## Hexagonal Architecture (Ports and Adapters)
    19→
    20→Isolate business logic from infrastructure through ports (interfaces) and adapters (implementations).
    21→
    22→- **Ports**: technology-agnostic interfaces for external communication
    23→- **Primary ports** (driving): REST controllers, CLI handlers, message consumers -- inbound
    24→- **Secondary ports** (driven): DB repos, external service clients, filesystem -- outbound
    25→- **Adapters**: technology-specific port implementations
    26→
    27→Benefits: testability (isolated core) | flexibility (swap infrastructure) | technology independence | maintainability
    28→
    29→Testing: unit tests through driving ports, mock driven ports | integration tests with real infrastructure | acceptance tests end-to-end through primary ports
    30→
    31→## Architectural Pattern Selection
    32→
    33→### Layered Architecture
    34→Horizontal layers with defined dependencies. Use for: traditional enterprise apps, clear separation. Trade-off: familiar but potential overhead, layer coupling.
    35→
    36→### Microservices
    37→Independent deployable services per capability. Use for: large teams, component scaling, tech diversity. Trade-off: scalability vs operational complexity. 2025 consensus: "start monolith, evolve when needed." Modular monolith = valid middle ground.
    38→
    39→### Event-Driven Architecture
    40→Components communicate via events through broker. Use for: real-time, complex processes, loose coupling. Trade-off: scalability/decoupling vs event ordering, debugging.
    41→
    42→### CQRS + Event Sourcing
    43→Separate read/write models; store events not state. Use for: financial, audit, temporal queries. Trade-off: complete history + independent scaling vs eventual consistency + complexity. NOT for: simple CRUD, strong consistency, inexperienced teams.
    44→
    45→## Domain-Driven Design (DDD)
    46→
    47→### Strategic Patterns
    48→- **Bounded Context**: explicit boundaries for domain model; prevents "single unified model" trap
    49→- **Context Mapping**: Shared Kernel | Customer/Supplier | Anti-Corruption Layer | Open Host Service
    50→
    51→### Tactical Patterns
    52→- **Aggregates**: consistency/transactional boundaries; root = only entry point
    53→- **Domain Events**: represent occurrences; enable loose coupling between contexts
    54→
    55→### Identifying Boundaries
    56→Language differences between departments | representation differences | consistency requirements define aggregate boundaries. Bounded contexts often map to microservice boundaries and team ownership.
    57→
    58→## ISO 25010 Quality Attributes
    59→
    60→Eight characteristics:
    61→1. **Functional Suitability**: completeness, correctness, appropriateness
    62→2. **Performance Efficiency**: time behavior, resource utilization, capacity
    63→3. **Compatibility**: coexistence, interoperability
    64→4. **Usability**: learnability, operability, accessibility
    65→5. **Reliability**: maturity, availability, fault tolerance, recoverability
    66→6. **Security**: confidentiality, integrity, non-repudiation, accountability, authenticity
    67→7. **Maintainability**: modularity, reusability, analyzability, modifiability, testability
    68→8. **Portability**: adaptability, installability, replaceability
    69→
    70→Trade-offs: Security vs Performance | Scalability vs Consistency (CAP) | Flexibility vs Performance | Usability vs Security
    71→
    72→Application: identify priority attributes, define measurable requirements, analyze trade-offs, validate with ATAM.
    73→
    74→## ATAM (Architecture Trade-off Analysis Method)
    75→
    76→Systematic evaluation from SEI/CMU.
    77→
    78→**Phase 1 - Presentation**: business drivers, architecture approaches, design decisions
    79→**Phase 2 - Investigation**: quality attribute scenarios, evaluate approaches, identify sensitivity/trade-off points
    80→**Phase 3 - Testing**: prioritize scenarios, analyze top in depth, document risks/non-risks
    81→
    82→Key concepts: **Sensitivity Point** (impacts one attribute) | **Trade-off Point** (affects multiple attributes) | **Architectural Risk** (may prevent attribute achievement)
    83→
    84→CBAM extends ATAM with economic analysis (ROI-driven). Perform early when cost of change is minimal. Lightweight: Mini-ATAM (half-day workshop).
    85→
    86→## Cloud Resilience Patterns
    87→
    88→### Circuit Breaker
    89→Monitor failures; after threshold fail fast ("open"); periodically test recovery. States: Closed, Open, Half-Open. Prevents cascading failures.
    90→
    91→### Retry with Exponential Backoff
    92→1s, 2s, 4s, 8s + jitter. Only transient errors, not business logic. Operations must be idempotent.
    93→
    94→### Bulkhead
    95→Isolate elements into pools; one failure doesn't affect others. Separate connection/thread pools per feature/tenant.
    96→
    97→### Throttling
    98→Rate limiting, concurrency limiting, resource quotas per user/tenant/service.
    99→
   100→### Saga Pattern
   101→Distributed transactions as local transaction sequence with compensating rollbacks. Choreography (decentralized) vs Orchestration (centralized).
   102→
   103→## API Architecture: REST vs GraphQL
   104→
   105→**REST**: resource-based URLs, HTTP verbs, stateless, standard caching. Best for: public APIs, simple CRUD, caching-critical.
   106→**GraphQL**: single endpoint, client-specified queries, typed schema. Best for: mobile (bandwidth), nested data, rapid frontend iteration.
   107→**Hybrid**: GraphQL gateway aggregating REST/RPC backends.
   108→Security for GraphQL: query depth limiting, complexity analysis, timeout, field-level auth.
   109→
   110→## ADR Templates
   111→
   112→**Nygard** (most common): Title, Status (Proposed/Accepted/Deprecated/Superseded), Context, Decision, Consequences
   113→**MADR** (extended): adds trade-off analysis, considered options with pros/cons
   114→**Y-Statement** (concise): "In context of [use case], facing [concern], decided for [option] to achieve [quality], accepting [downside]"
   115→
   116→Best practices: single decision per ADR | immutable (supersede, never modify) | store in VCS | create when decided
   117→
   118→## Technology Selection: Open Source Priority
   119→
   120→Evaluation order: 1. Mature OSS with strong community | 2. Newer OSS with active dev | 3. Proprietary only when specified or no viable OSS
   121→
   122→OSS criteria: last commit <6 months | regular releases | quick issue resolution | 10+ regular contributors | >1000 GitHub stars for critical components
   123→
   124→License preference: MIT > Apache 2.0 > BSD > MPL 2.0 > LGPL (caution) > GPL (careful) > AGPL (extreme caution). Proprietary forbidden without explicit request.
   125→
   126→Document per selection: name/version, license, GitHub URL/stats, maintenance assessment, alternatives considered.
   127→

1→---
     2→name: roadmap-design
     3→description: Roadmap concision rules, step decomposition efficiency, AC abstraction guidelines, and step-to-scenario mapping. Load when creating implementation roadmaps.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# Roadmap Design
     7→
     8→## Canonical Format
     9→
    10→Use ONLY compact nested format. See `nWave/templates/roadmap-schema.yaml` for fields/validation. Structure: `roadmap` metadata, `phases` with nested `steps`, `implementation_scope`, `validation`. Phase IDs: two digits (`"01"`). Step IDs: `"NN-MM"`.
    11→
    12→## Concision Requirements
    13→
    14→Token efficiency: crafter reads roadmap ~35 times (7 phases x 5 steps). 5000-token x 35 = 175k tokens. 1000-token x 35 = 35k (80% savings).
    15→
    16→### Quantitative Limits
    17→- Step description: max 50 words | AC per step: max 5 | Each AC: max 30 words
    18→- Step notes: max 100 words
    19→- Target: small (1-3 steps) = 500, medium (4-8) = 1500, large (9-15) = 3000 tokens
    20→
    21→### Include
    22→WHAT to build (one sentence) | WHY (brief business value) | Observable outcomes (testable AC) | Architectural constraints | Integration points
    23→
    24→### Exclude
    25→HOW to implement | code snippets/class names | algorithm descriptions | step-by-step instructions | tech tutorials | testing strategy (TDD standard) | motivational language | redundancy | examples when description clear
    26→
    27→### Compression Techniques
    28→Bullets over prose | eliminate qualifiers | assume expertise | active voice | omit obvious
    29→
    30→## Acceptance Criteria Abstraction
    31→
    32→AC describe observable outcomes, never internal implementation.
    33→
    34→### Rules
    35→Describe WHAT (behavior) not HOW (implementation) | Never reference private methods (underscore prefix) | Never reference internal class decomposition | Never prescribe signatures/params/return types | Crafter decides structure during GREEN + REFACTOR
    36→
    37→### Good AC Examples
    38→- "DES module importable from installation target after install"
    39→- "DES utility scripts present and executable in scripts directory"
    40→- "Installation uses backup before overwriting existing components"
    41→
    42→### Bad AC Examples (implementation-coupled)
    43→- "_install_des_module() copies src/des/ to ~/.claude/lib/python/des/"
    44→- "verify() checks DES module importable via subprocess test"
    45→- "context.installation_verifier._check_agents() called for verification"
    46→
    47→## Step Decomposition Efficiency
    48→
    49→### Step Ratio Check
    50→Rule: `steps_count / estimated_production_files <= 2.5`
    51→Violation = over-decomposition. Action: merge steps targeting same file.
    52→
    53→### Identical Pattern Detection
    54→Rule: if N steps differ only by substitution variable, batch into 1 step. Threshold: 3+ identical-pattern steps must batch.
    55→Bad: 4 separate steps for AgentsPlugin, CommandsPlugin, TemplatesPlugin, UtilitiesPlugin
    56→Good: 1 step creating all 4
    57→
    58→### No-Op Prevention
    59→Each step must add production code. Validation-only steps are not steps.
    60→Bad: "Step 02-05: Validate extraction with integration tests"
    61→Good: validation is part of preceding step's REVIEW phase
    62→
    63→## Step-to-Scenario Mapping
    64→
    65→For roadmaps feeding DELIVER wave:
    66→1. Read acceptance tests (tests/acceptance/test_*.py) before creating roadmap
    67→2. Count scenarios (def test_*) | 3. ~1 step per scenario (flexibility for infra steps)
    68→4. Mark infra steps: "type: infrastructure" | 5. Principle: 1 Scenario = 1 Step = 1 TDD Cycle
    69→
    70→## Measure Before Plan Gate
    71→
    72→Before any roadmap, verify: 1. Timing data shows WHERE time spent | 2. Impact ranking shows MOST contributing component | 3. Target validation provides evidence
    73→
    74→Missing data: halt, request measurement, offer to help. Gate is blocking.
    75→
    76→## Simplest Solution Gate
    77→
    78→Before multi-phase (>3 steps), document rejected alternatives:
    79→
    80→### Alternatives to Consider
    81→1. Configuration-only (no code) | 2. Single-file change | 3. Existing tool/library | 4. Partial (solve 80% simply)
    82→
    83→### Documentation Format
    84→```markdown
    85→## Rejected Simple Alternatives
    86→
    87→### Alternative 1: {simplest approach}
    88→- What: {description}
    89→- Expected Impact: {% of problem solved}
    90→- Why Insufficient: {evidence-based reason}
    91→
    92→### Why Complex Solution Necessary
    93→1. Simple alternatives fail due to: {specific reason with evidence}
    94→2. Complexity justified by: {benefit simple solutions cannot achieve}
    95→```
    96→
    97→Min 2 alternatives. Each: specific description, expected impact, evidence-based rejection.
    98→

1→import { RecordId, Surreal } from "surrealdb";
     2→import type { SuggestionSummary } from "../../../shared/contracts";

     7→function formatProjects(rows: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof listWorkspaceProjectSummaries>>): string {
     8→  if (rows.length === 0) {
     9→    return "- none";
    10→  }
    11→
    12→  return rows
    13→    .slice(0, 20)
    14→    .map((row) => `- ${row.name} [id: ${row.id}] active tasks: ${row.activeTaskCount}`)
    15→    .join("\n");
    16→}
    17→
    18→function formatSuggestions(rows: SuggestionSummary[]): string {
    19→  if (rows.length === 0) {
    20→    return "- none";
    21→  }
    22→
    23→  return rows
    24→    .slice(0, 20)
    25→    .map((row) => `- [${row.category}] ${row.text} (${row.status}, confidence ${row.confidence.toFixed(2)}, by ${row.suggestedBy})`)
    26→    .join("\n");
    27→}
    28→
    29→function formatObservations(rows: Awaited<ReturnType<typeof listWorkspaceOpenObservations>>): string {
    30→  if (rows.length === 0) {
    31→    return "- none";
    32→  }
    33→
    34→  return rows
    35→    .slice(0, 20)
    36→    .map((row) => {
    37→      const category = row.category ? `, ${row.category}` : "";
    38→      return `- [${row.severity}] ${row.text} (${row.status}, by ${row.sourceAgent}${category})`;
    39→    })
    40→    .join("\n");
    41→}

    60→      surreal: input.surreal,
    61→      workspaceRecord: input.workspaceRecord,
    62→      limit: 20,
    63→    }),
    64→  ]);
    65→
    66→  return [
    67→    "You are the Product Manager subagent.",
    68→    "You are the single authority on tasks, features, projects, and project status.",
    69→    "",
    70→    "## Architecture: Graph as Communication Bus",
    71→    "All agent-to-agent communication happens through the knowledge graph.",
    72→    "Never rely on handoff text from other agents; read context from graph tools.",
    73→    "Other agents (Research, Engineering, Marketing, etc.) write to the same graph.",
    74→    "You may discover entities and observations created by other agents when querying.",
    75→    "Your observations are visible to all agents in subsequent invocations.",
    76→    "",

    78→    ...(workspace?.name ? [`The workspace "${workspace.name}" is the business/brand — strip "${workspace.name}" from project names (e.g. "${workspace.name} Dashboard" → "Dashboard").`] : []),
    79→    "- Project: named product area, module, or workstream within the workspace (e.g. Dashboard, Inventory, Orders)",
    80→    "- Feature: capability/requirement WITHIN an existing project (supports, provides, enables, needs to handle)",
    81→    "- Task: concrete executable work with action verb (implement, build, fix, migrate, deploy, test)",
    82→    "",
    83→    "**HARD RULE: Features and tasks MUST belong to a project. If Workspace Projects below shows '- none', you MUST create the project FIRST in a SEPARATE tool call step — return ONLY the create_work_item(kind:'project') call, wait for it to complete, THEN create features/tasks in the next step. Never create a project and its children in the same tool call batch. A user-described heading with a description is a PROJECT, not a feature.**",
    84→    "",
    85→    "## Reorganizing Existing Items",
    86→    "When the user says items belong under a different project (e.g. 'X is a separate project with these features'), use move_items_to_project to reassign them. Do NOT create duplicates.",
    87→    "Steps: (1) search_entities to find the existing items, (2) create the new project if needed, (3) move_items_to_project with the entity IDs and target project.",
    88→    "",
    89→    "## When to Create Directly vs Suggest",
